query:
INSERT INTO public.vocabulary_words (word, definition, example_sentence, part_of_speech, difficulty, exam_type)
VALUES
  ('Preamble', 'An introductory statement', 'The essay highlights the preamble of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Precarious', 'Not secure; risky or unstable', '“His financial situation was precarious after losing his job.”', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Precaution', 'A measure taken in advance to secure good results', 'The author hoped the new policy would precaution tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Precede', 'To go before, go in front of', 'The author hoped the new policy would precede tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Precedence', 'The condition of being more important than something else', '“In emergencies, safety takes precedence over convenience.”', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Precedent', 'An earlier event serving as an example', 'The essay highlights the precedent of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Precept', 'A commandment given as a rule of action or conduct', 'Her precept became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Precession', 'Precedence', 'Scholars noted a striking precession between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Precinct', 'A district in a city marked out for government purposes', 'Her precinct became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Precipice', 'A very steep cliff', 'Her precipice became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Precipitant', 'Falling headlong', 'The essay highlights the precipitant of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Precipitous', 'Dangerously high or steep; sudden and dramatic', '“The company’s precipitous decline worried investors.”', 'adjective', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Precise', 'Exact, accurate', 'Her precise became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Precision', 'Accuracy', 'The essay highlights the precision of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Preclude', 'To prevent from happening', '“His injury precluded him from playing in the final game.”', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Precocious', 'Having developed certain abilities at an earlier age than usual', '“The precocious child could read at age three.”', 'adjective', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Preconceive', 'Formed before having full knowledge or experience', '“She entered the debate with a preconceived notion of who was right.”', 'adjective', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Precursor', 'A person or thing that comes before another', '“The pager was a precursor to the modern smartphone.”', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Predatory', 'Of, or relating to a predator', 'The author hoped the new policy would predatory tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Predecessor', 'Something replaced by something else', 'The essay highlights the predecessor of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Predetermine', 'Decided or set in advance', '“The outcome of the competition seemed predetermined.”', 'adjective', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Predicament', 'A difficult, perplexing or dangerous situation', 'The essay highlights the predicament of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Predictive', 'Relating to the ability to predict future events', '“The scientist used predictive models to forecast climate changes.”', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Predilection', 'A preference or liking for something', 'Scholars noted a striking predilection between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Predispose', 'To make someone more likely to behave in a certain way', '“Genetics can predispose individuals to certain diseases.”', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Predominance', 'The condition or state of having power over others', 'Her predominance became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Preeminence', 'The status of being preeminent, dominant or ascendant', 'Her preeminence became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Preeminent', 'Exceeding others in quality or rank', 'The reviewer described the tone as preeminent, which shaped how readers interpreted the claim.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Preempt', 'Take action to prevent something', 'Over time, the community learned to preempt its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Preengage', 'To engage beforehand', 'Over time, the community learned to preengage its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Preestablish', 'To establish beforehand', 'The author hoped the new policy would preestablish tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Preexist', 'To exist before something else', 'The author hoped the new policy would preexist tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Preface', 'An introductory part, or preliminary statement', 'Scholars noted a striking preface between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Prefatory', 'Introductory, preliminary, serving as a prelude or preface', 'Her prefatory became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Prefix', 'An affix placed before a word to modify a term''s meaning', 'Over time, the community learned to prefix its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Prehensile', 'Able to take hold of and clasp objects', 'Over time, the community learned to prehensile its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Prehension', 'The act of grasping or gripping something', 'The essay highlights the prehension of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Prejudice', 'An unfavourable opinion formed without knowledge or reason', 'Scholars noted a striking prejudice between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Prelacy', 'The office of a prelate', 'Scholars noted a striking prelacy between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Prelate', 'A church dignitary', 'Her prelate became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Prelude', 'Any action or event that precedes something else', 'Her prelude became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Premature', 'Occurring too soon', 'Scholars noted a striking premature between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Premeditat', 'Done deliberately, planned in advance', 'The essay highlights the premeditat of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Premier', 'The head of the cabinet in some countries', 'Scholars noted a striking premier between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Premise', 'A proposition supporting or helping to support a conclusion', 'The author hoped the new policy would premise tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Premonition', 'A strong feeling that something is about to happen', 'The author hoped the new policy would premonition tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Preoccupy', 'To distract, to occupy or draw attention elsewhere', 'Critics argued that the plan might preoccupy the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Preordain', 'To determine the fate of something in advance', 'Critics argued that the plan might preordain the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Preparation', 'The act of getting ready for something', 'Her preparation became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Preponderance', 'Excess or superiority of influence', 'Her preponderance became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type)
ON CONFLICT (word, exam_type) DO UPDATE SET
  definition = EXCLUDED.definition,
  example_sentence = EXCLUDED.example_sentence,
  part_of_speech = EXCLUDED.part_of_speech,
  difficulty = EXCLUDED.difficulty;
